{"id":"project-LICHEN-65td","title":"senml: dec_int wrapping arithmetic may produce incorrect values","description":"File: rust/lichen-senml/src/cbor.rs
Line: 223

The dec_int function uses wrapping_sub for negative CBOR integers:
```rust
1 => Ok(((-(val as i64)).wrapping_sub(1), adv)),
```

For CBOR major type 1, the value encodes -(n+1), so we need to compute -1 - n. The current code:
1. Casts u64 to i64 (if val > i64::MAX, this wraps to negative)
2. Negates (could overflow if the wrapped value is i64::MIN)
3. Subtracts 1 with wrapping

When val is large (e.g., 0x8000_0000_0000_0000 or higher from a 64-bit CBOR int), this produces incorrect results silently instead of erroring.

Note: Currently dec_head only supports 16-bit values (max 65535), so this is not exploitable today. However:
- The function signature suggests it handles general i64
- If dec_head is later extended to support 32/64-bit, this becomes exploitable
- Defense in depth suggests validating now

Impact: Currently mitigated by dec_head limitation; future risk if dec_head extended.

Fix: Add explicit check before casting:
```rust
1 => {
    if val > i64::MAX as u64 {
        return Err(CborError::InvalidInput);
    }
    Ok((-(val as i64) - 1, adv))
}
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:39:31.239471Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:39:31.239507Z"}
